2604.26726.title: Swap distance minimization shapes the order of subject, object and verb in languages of the world
2604.26726.paper_summary: Languages of the world vary concerning the order of subject, object and verb. The most frequent dominant orders are SOV and SVO, and researchers have tailored models to this fact. However, there are still languages whose dominant order does not conform to these expectations or even lack a dominant order. Here we show that across linguistic families and macroareas, word order variation within languages is shaped by the principle of swap distance minimization even when the dominant order is not SOV/SVO and even when a dominant order is lacking.
2604.26726.paper_summary_zh: 世界的語言在主語、賓語和動詞的順序上有所不同。最常見的主要順序是SOV和SVO，研究人員已根據這一事實調整了模型。然後，仍然存在一些語言，其主要順序不符合這些預期，甚至缺乏主要順序。在這裡，我們展示了在語言家族和宏觀區域之間，語言內部的詞序變異是由交換距離最小化原則所塑造，即使主要順序不是SOV/SVO，甚至在缺乏主要順序的情況下也是如此。
2604.26726.author: Jairo Rios-El-Yazidi et.al.
